title: Complete a Circuit on Gas Stations
description:
Given There are n gas stations arranged in a circle. You have gas[i] units of gas at station i, and cost[i] to travel from i to i+1. Find the starting station index from which you can complete the circuit once in the clockwise direction. Return -1 if impossible. If a solution exists, it is unique.

    

Constraints:
    - 1 ≤ n ≤ 10^5
- 0 ≤ gas[i], cost[i] ≤ 10^4
explanation:
Intuition
If total gas < total cost there is no solution. Otherwise the unique start is found by a single pass: maintain a tank, and whenever tank drops negative at i, the next index must be the new candidate start.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Check total sum feasibility
2. Main Algorithm: Traverse stations, accumulate tank+=gas[i]-cost[i]; if tank<0, reset start=i+1 and tank=0
3. Return Result: Return start

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n)
- Space Complexity: O(1)

Key Insights
- Greedy reset works because any start within a failed segment fails
- Uniqueness follows from total surplus property

Common Pitfalls
- Forgetting feasibility check leads to wrong answers
- Reset start only when tank goes negative
hints: Compare total gas and total cost first
Reset start when tank goes negative
